intro: Gouldia rotundifolia, also known as Round-leaved Gouldia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae, endemic to the island of Moorea in French Polynesia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gouldia rotundifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from the plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat respiratory infections and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
